summary: Complete backend + frontend testing of TheDayLaborers new features. All 40 backend tests pass (100%). Frontend tested: landing page, crew dashboard (online toggle, GPS toggle, profile completion), contractor dashboard (post job, emergency job, duplicate job, crew search with location, crew cards View/Share/Request), subscription page (CashApp Pay, annual plan). Two code bugs found and reported.

minor.issue: UserCreate model missing 'trade' field. Trade selected during crew registration is never saved to the user document. auth_routes.py always sets trade='' in user_doc regardless of input. Profile completion Trade/Skills check is always False at registration.

ui_bugs.issue: STATUS_LABELS defined in ContractorDashboard.jsx ('open'→'Posted', 'fulfilled'→'Accepted', 'in_progress'→'In Progress', 'completed_pending_review'→'Completed', 'completed'→'Verified') are NEVER passed to or used by JobCard.jsx. JobCard displays raw status via {job.status?.replace('_', ' ')} showing 'open', 'fulfilled', 'in progress' instead of the intended human-readable labels.
New crew members show '0.0' rating instead of '—' or 'New'. user?.rating?.toFixed(1) returns '0.0' (truthy) so || '—' never triggers. Should check rating_count > 0 before showing numeric rating.

issues: Uses browser native datetime-local picker for job start time (carried from iteration 1)

tests: Profile completion returns percentage + 5 checks (photo, phone, address, skills, bio)
Crew search by trade, name, address
Job duplication → 201, title has (Copy), crew_accepted=[], is_emergency=false
Second accept on filled emergency job → 409
Full job workflow: accept → start → complete → verify → status=completed
Payment plans include annual plan ($699.99, 365 days)
All plan amounts correct (daily=4.99, weekly=24.99, monthly=79.99, annual=699.99)
Subscription gating: expired user cannot post jobs → 403 + SUBSCRIPTION_EXPIRED

flows: Landing page loads with hero, nav, CTA buttons
Auth page: register crew/contractor with role selection
Crew dashboard: GPS toggle present ('Enable GPS')
Crew dashboard: Profile completion panel at 20% with 5 checklist items
Crew dashboard: Complete Profile link navigates to profile
Contractor dashboard: Post Job button + job creation form
Contractor dashboard: Emergency job toggle ('Send Emergency Alert' button text)
Contractor dashboard: Job duplication (Copy icon, 'Job duplicated and reposted!' toast)
Contractor dashboard: Crew search with name/trade/location fields + Search Crew button
Contractor dashboard: Crew cards with View/Share/Request buttons
Subscription page: 4 plans (daily/weekly/monthly/annual), CashApp Pay default, annual=$699.99

action_items: FIX: Add 'trade: Optional[str] = None' to UserCreate model in models.py and use 'data.trade or ''  in auth_routes.py user_doc to save trade during registration
FIX: Pass STATUS_LABELS to JobCard.jsx or use them inside JobCard - show 'Posted' instead of 'open', 'Accepted' instead of 'fulfilled', 'Verified' instead of 'completed'
MINOR: Fix crew member Rating display - show 'New' instead of '0.0' for users with rating_count == 0
critical_code_review_comments: MEDIUM: UserCreate model (models.py) missing 'trade' field → trade selected in registration form never saved → Skills/Trade profile completion check always starts as False even if user selected a trade
MEDIUM: STATUS_LABELS in ContractorDashboard.jsx is dead code - never passed to or used by JobCard.jsx. Jobs show raw 'open'/'fulfilled'/'in_progress' instead of 'Posted'/'Accepted'/'In Progress'
LOW: New crew member rating shows '0.0' instead of 'New/—' because rating=0.0 (falsy check on number fails)
INFO: Square payment uses production credentials and returns real square.link short URLs - working correctly
INFO: WebSocket has 2 retry attempts before connecting on initial load - not critical, reconnects successfully
INFO: PayPal client ID hardcoded in SubscriptionPage.jsx (public client ID, acceptable for frontend)

success_rate.frontend: 100% (all critical flows work, 2 UX bugs found)

seed_data_creation: Multiple TEST_ prefixed users and jobs created during testing. Crew users: uitest_crew_*, crewtest_*, subtest_*. Contractors: contractor_test_*, duptest_*.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All new P0 features tested and working: subscription gating, profile completion, online toggle, GPS toggle, crew search with address, job duplication, emergency job race lock, job workflow, Square/CashApp payment, annual plan. Two medium bugs found: (1) trade not saved at registration, (2) STATUS_LABELS not used in JobCard. Backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_new_features.py (40 tests, 100% passing).

trade_not_saved.bug: Trade selected at crew registration is never saved
trade_not_saved.root_cause: UserCreate pydantic model (models.py line 17-23) does not include 'trade' field. auth_routes.py user_doc always sets 'trade': '' hardcoded. Frontend sends trade in payload (AuthPage.jsx line 41-43) but backend ignores it.
trade_not_saved.reproduction: Register as crew, select a trade, check /api/users/profile-completion → skills check is always False
trade_not_saved.fix: 1. Add 'trade: Optional[str] = None' to UserCreate model. 2. In auth_routes.py user_doc, change '"trade": ""' to '"trade": data.trade or ""'

status_labels_unused.bug: Job status shows raw database values instead of user-friendly labels
status_labels_unused.root_cause: STATUS_LABELS dict defined in ContractorDashboard.jsx but never used. JobCard.jsx uses {job.status?.replace('_', ' ')} directly.
status_labels_unused.reproduction: Post a job as contractor → status shows 'open' not 'Posted'
status_labels_unused.fix: Either pass statusLabel prop to JobCard, or define STATUS_LABELS inside JobCard.jsx and use it for display
